name: Laptop
description: Personal computers including MacBooks, Windows laptops, Chromebooks, and tablets. Laptops are among the most commonly carried electronic devices on flights.
tsaGuideline: Laptops must be removed from bags and placed in a separate bin during TSA security screening. Laptops are allowed in both carry-on and checked baggage, but carry-on is strongly recommended due to theft risk and TSA lithium battery guidelines.
iataGuideline: Portable electronic devices including laptops are permitted in cabin baggage. IATA recommends keeping devices with lithium batteries in the cabin.
restrictions: Must be removed from carry-on bag at TSA checkpoint and placed in separate bin
Lithium battery inside laptop: allowed in cabin; carry-on recommended over checked
Smart laptops or laptops with large non-standard batteries may need airline approval

airlineRules.notes: Laptop allowed in carry-on. Remove from bag at TSA.
Permitted in carry-on. Recommended to keep in cabin for lithium battery safety.
Allowed in carry-on and checked. Remove at TSA.
Permitted in cabin baggage. Comply with local security authority rules.

faqs.question: Do I have to take my laptop out at airport security?
faqs.answer: Yes, in the United States the TSA requires you to remove laptops from your bag and place them in a separate bin for X-ray screening. TSA PreCheck members do not need to remove laptops. Rules vary by country.

faqs.question: Can I put my laptop in checked luggage?
faqs.answer: Technically yes, but it is strongly discouraged. Laptops can be damaged, stolen, or have batteries damaged in the cargo hold. TSA and airlines recommend keeping laptops in your carry-on bag.

faqs.question: Is there a size limit for laptops on flights?
faqs.answer: There is no specific size restriction for laptops from TSA or IATA, but the laptop must fit within your carry-on bag allowance. Very large 17-inch gaming laptops may be difficult to fit depending on your bag.
